title: Purged race-blocked rank stacker with a monotone physics expert
summary: Grouped leakage literature implies adjacent laps from the same driver-race can make CV too optimistic, especially once you add encoders or stackers.
rationale: Grouped leakage literature implies adjacent laps from the same driver-race can make CV too optimistic, especially once you add encoders or stackers. A small monotone expert can regularize the most physics-like relationships while rank-only stacking stays aligned with AUC.
implementation_hint: Use race-blocked or walk-forward folds with an embargo/gap around held-out races; train base models as the current GBDT, the pairwise ranker, and a monotone CatBoost/XGBoost specialist on features like latent wear, tyre life, laps remaining, and rule-based stop debt; stack only OOF percentile ranks.
expected_effect: Usually lowers optimistic local variance and can improve public robustness by combining diverse inductive biases without over-trusting fold-specific probabilities.
risk: Stricter CV may make local scores look worse before public scores improve; monotone constraints hurt if applied to the wrong features or too broadly.
